raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":1.0,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.5","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"Improving the draft centrally includes strengthening the thesis, gap, and contribution framing above the sentence level.","R1.2":"Improving the draft requires checking whether the argument progresses coherently from problem motivation to method and claimed contribution.","R1.3":"Because the draft reports dataset results, cost reductions, and improvement percentages, aligning claims with reported evidence is a central responsibility.","R1.5":"The request implies attention to the paragraph and section-level narrative arc of the research draft.","R1.6":"Writing polish is load-bearing because the delegation is a broad request to improve the draft itself.","RX.1":"Any projected improvement responsibility must preserve uncertainty around unverifiable empirical, citation, and novelty claims.","RX.2":"The draft contains strong claims about canonical baselines, relative gains, and deployment pathways, making overclaim avoidance active.","RX.3":"The agent must stay within improving the draft rather than executing experiments, verifying external literature, or rewriting beyond the delegation.","RX.4":"Battery management claims can affect safety-relevant downstream interpretations, so harm avoidance remains active.","RX.5":"Changes or assessments should remain traceable to the provided artifact and distinguish reported facts from inferred improvements."}}

rationale.R1.1: Improving the draft centrally includes strengthening the thesis, gap, and contribution framing above the sentence level.
rationale.R1.2: Improving the draft requires checking whether the argument progresses coherently from problem motivation to method and claimed contribution.
rationale.R1.3: Because the draft reports dataset results, cost reductions, and improvement percentages, aligning claims with reported evidence is a central responsibility.
rationale.R1.5: The request implies attention to the paragraph and section-level narrative arc of the research draft.
rationale.R1.6: Writing polish is load-bearing because the delegation is a broad request to improve the draft itself.
rationale.RX.1: Any projected improvement responsibility must preserve uncertainty around unverifiable empirical, citation, and novelty claims.
rationale.RX.2: The draft contains strong claims about canonical baselines, relative gains, and deployment pathways, making overclaim avoidance active.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must stay within improving the draft rather than executing experiments, verifying external literature, or rewriting beyond the delegation.
rationale.RX.4: Battery management claims can affect safety-relevant downstream interpretations, so harm avoidance remains active.
rationale.RX.5: Changes or assessments should remain traceable to the provided artifact and distinguish reported facts from inferred improvements.
